description.content: Operations related to agent management

query.description: Maximum number of records to return. Must be between 1 and 100 inclusive.
Number of records to skip for pagination. Must be a non-negative integer.

Filter agents by a custom attribute value. Only keys inside the `attributes` JSON
object are filterable; system fields (`name`, `description`, `type`, `ouId`, `owner`,
`clientId`) are not supported.
Supported operators: `eq`.
Format: `attribute operator "value"`. The value must be wrapped in double quotes.
Example: `region eq "us-east-1"`.
Pass `display` to populate `ouHandle` fields in the response.

Creates a new agent. At minimum, an entity is created with the provided identity fields.
An inbound auth profile is created when any inbound auth config fields are supplied.
An OAuth profile is created when `inboundAuthConfig` is supplied.

The `clientSecret` is returned once in the response and never again — store it securely.

response.name: Agent created

Full replacement update. `name` and `type` are required. All inbound auth profile
and OAuth profile fields are applied according to the same rules as
CreateAgentRequest. Omitting an inbound auth or OAuth field clears it.

If `ouId` is provided and differs from the current value, the agent is moved to the
new organization unit (which must exist).

Returns a paginated list of groups the agent is a member of. Use the group and role APIs
to add the agent to groups or assign roles directly.

Operations related to agent type schema management

Retrieves the list of agent type schemas. Agent types are restricted to a single
bootstrap-provisioned `default` schema; this endpoint returns that one schema.

query.description: Maximum number of records to return. Must be between 1 and 100 inclusive.
Number of records to skip for pagination. Must be a non-negative integer.
Pass `display` to populate `ouHandle` fields in the response.

item.name: Create the default agent type schema

request.name: Create the default agent type schema

description.content:
Creates the agent type schema. The `name` must be `default` — agent types are
restricted to a single `default` schema per deployment, so any other name is
rejected with `USRS-1014`.

item.name: Update the default agent type schema by ID

request.name: Update the default agent type schema by ID

description.content:
Updates the agent type schema with new attribute definitions. The `name` must remain
`default` — renaming to anything else is rejected with `USRS-1014`. This operation
replaces the entire schema definition.

item.name: Delete an agent type schema by ID (not supported)

request.name: Delete an agent type schema by ID (not supported)

description.content:
Deleting agent type schemas is not supported. The `default` agent type cannot be
removed; agent creation depends on it. Update the schema in place instead.
This endpoint always returns `USRS-1015`.
